frameworks.name: synaptic plasticity theory of memory
frameworks.one_line: Synapses are storage sites modified associatively to form memories.

frameworks.name: intracellular molecular memory
frameworks.one_line: Memory is stored in cell-body molecules via biochemical operations.

frameworks.name: computational theory of memory (Bayesian latent-cause inference)
frameworks.one_line: Memory implements approximate posterior inference over latent causes given a generative model.

core_question: How can synaptic and intracellular accounts of memory storage be reconciled within a single computational theory?
key_assumptions: memory can be cast as learning and inference over latent causes
an approximate posterior distribution and a generative model are separable computational objects
synapses and intracellular molecules can carry distinct parameter roles
interpretations: synapses store parameters of an approximate posterior over latent causes
intracellular molecules store parameters of the generative model, and both jointly implement learning and inference
